bullets: Chinese PP rises on raw-material costs while SEA keeps falling.
Indonesia proposes ADD up to 17.32% on Vietnamese PP homo.
Sabic LDPE coating to 1,550 (5+ ctn); LLDPE Film to 1,155 CIF HCMC.
Chinese coal-based LLDPE to a low 32,700; merchants undercut LSP.
Regional PE -30-50 across segments on weak low-season demand.
China oil-based PVC -300 RMB; buyers shift to coal-based.
Chinese coal-based SG-5 held 22,000-22,300; oil-based to 24,000.

editorial.changedNote: A few HDPE/LDPE items eased; PVC mostly flat

summary: Sabic slashed LDPE coating to 1,550 and LLDPE Film to 1,155 CIF to fight slow sales.
Oversupply returned, dragging Chinese coal-based LLDPE to a low 32,700 as merchants undercut LSP.
Asian PE fell sharply (30-50 across segments) on weak low-season demand.
paras: On June 4, Sabic finalized LDPE coating 1,630 (under 5 ctn) / 1,550 (over 5 ctn, down 70); LLDPE Film 118NJ/218WJA/222WJ at 1,155 CIF HCMC (down 45-65) - slow consumption.
ME supply rose: Borouge LDPE 1,530; Oman LLDPE 1,190-1,200; Basell US-made LDPE 1,530 (competitive).
Lotte held PP/PE high: LLDPE injection 1,400, HDPE yarn 1,450, Hyundai HDPE Film 1,320 - seen high.

Domestic 7042 (Kunlun)/7050 at 34,400 (down 100-300); Baofeng coal-based 32,700; another 33,400; Sabic-China/Sabic 222WJ 36,500; ZPC 2426H 45,000 / 42,800. Qamar FD21HS/218WJ 36,300-36,400 (down 500).

LSP held LLDPE Film 38,200, HDPE Film 41,200 South - too high; merchants undercut. Regional: NEA HDPE -50, LLDPE -35, LDPE -40; SEA HDPE -30 to 1,310-1,320, LLDPE -30 to 1,270, mC6 -30 to 1,420, LDPE -30 to 1,520.

summary: Asian PP diverged: Chinese domestic rose on raw-material costs while SEA kept falling.
Indonesia proposed ADD up to 17.32% on Vietnamese PP homo, threatening exports.
Domestic stayed oversupplied and discounted as NSRP readied new spot sales.
paras: Per Platts on June 4, NEA PP flat; SEA yarn/injection -30 to 1,240, BOPP -20 to 1,270, IPP/block copo -30 to 1,290 (Vietnam 30-40 below).
Chinese domestic PP rose ~100 RMB in three days on high feedstock; suppliers favored domestic sales over exports amid Vietnamese/Korean competition and weak SEA demand (Indonesia holidays).
Indonesia KADI proposed ADD 5.52-36.62% on PP homo from 8 countries; Vietnamese makers (BSR, NSRP) at 17.32%, Hyosung Vina 7.07%.
NSRP readied a 15,000-ton 114G/105G spot sale (June 10-30); inventory high, run rate reportedly up to 120%.

Chinese offers mixed: some homo from 1,280, block copo from 1,380 (up), Jinneng HP550J as high as 1,365, then Jinneng cut to 1,175 FOB Qingdao (BOPP 1,185, down 25). Lotte Hyundai PP yarn 1,250, Lotte Chemical 1,330 - high.

LSP held PP homo 38,200-38,300, copo 39,100-39,200 South; merchants lower. Stavian BSR T3034 36,400, Jinneng HP550J 35,800, MP9600 49,000. North copo K8003/K8009 39,800-40,000, non-woven Y40 40,500, yarn 1102K 38,900. NSRP 114G lowest ~34,500 (500 below last-week lows).

summary: Asian PVC kept falling, led by a 300 RMB drop in Chinese oil-based as buyers shifted to coal-based.
Vietnam held Chinese coal-based at 22,000-22,300 while oil-based fell to 24,000.
Southern sellers undercut factory rates, finalizing domestic/Thai PVC at 24,000-24,500.
paras: Per Platts, China PVC -10 to 850 CFR; SEA -10 to 800 CFR. China oil-based fell ~300 RMB while coal-based held.
Asian PVC drifted sideways to lower on weak demand and oil-to-coal substitution; the rainy low season nears.
In Vietnam, North Xinfa SG-5 at 22,000 with transport, Yihua 22,300 - sideways. Southern Yihua SG-5 22,000; Chinese oil-based to 24,000.
Southern domestic/Thai SG660/SP660/PM66R finalized 24,000-24,500, below factory rates, on cautious sentiment.
